_purpose: FROZEN before looking at outcomes. A NEW pre-registered test (not the onset Gate-0): does temporal DS characterize post-fire RECOVERY / seasonal-dynamics disruption in a way that (a) is sustained (unlike a single NBR step), (b) beats the conventional min-angle, and (c) is NOT just re-deriving NBR (circularity guard)?
pre_event_ref_window: last 24 valid dates ending >30d before event

circularity_guard.rule: DS uses bands EXCLUDING NBR's B8/B12 (main) and also a strict visible-only variant. A non-circular positive requires DS-recovery to track NBR-recovery while built from different bands, AND to beat a raw-mean-reflectance baseline.

preregistered_claims.C1_sustained: post-fire d_pre(t) stays elevated for >= 90 days (a sustained dynamics disruption), vs control AOI which does not.
preregistered_claims.C2_noncircular_value: d_pre (B8/B12-excluded) correlates with the NBR recovery trend AND does so better than a raw-mean-reflectance baseline (else DS adds nothing).
preregistered_claims.C3_beats_minangle: full DS d_pre separates burn-vs-control more than the single min-angle does (the verified-regime claim, on real data).
preregistered_claims.C4_control_quiet: unburned control AOI shows no sustained d_pre elevation around the event.

falsifier: If d_pre on burn is not sustained-elevated vs control, OR DS adds nothing over raw reflectance for recovery tracking, OR full DS does not beat min-angle -> NEGATIVE; commit to the diagnostic paper.
no_tuning_rule: These choices are frozen. Any exploration happens only on the control / a held-out AOI, never tuned to make the burn result look better.
